cover_caption: Overview of PP-Tac. The system leverages tactile feedback from the proposed round-shaped sensor (R-Tac), integrated into a dexterous robotic hand, to grasp thin, deformable, paper-like objects. (a) Hand motions are generated by a diffusion-based policy inspired by human strategies, such as sliding and pinching. (b) The hardware setup includes a robotic arm, a dexterous hand, and four fingertip-mounted tactile sensors that simultaneously detect force and slip events.

ai_summary.one_liner: Tactile-feedback dexterous hand system that picks paper-like thin deformable objects via R-Tac slip detection + online force control + diffusion policy, achieving 87.5% success rate
ai_summary.problem: Robotic hands cannot reliably grasp thin, flat, deformable objects like paper and fabric due to insufficient perception for slip/force state estimation and lack of appropriate grasp motion planning
ai_summary.solution: PP-Tac: dexterous hand with 4 round-shaped high-resolution tactile sensors (R-Tac) + slip detection CNN + online frictional force control + diffusion-based policy trained on synthesized pinching trajectories
ai_summary.key_result: 87.5% overall success rate across 4 objects (paper, plastic bag, cloth, paper bag) and 4 terrains (plane, slope, book, random)
limitations: Constrained to specific dexterous hand morphology and fingertip-mounted sensor form factor
Trajectory synthesis assumes specific contact geometry (buckling model) that may not generalize to 3D deformable objects

figures.caption: Overview of PP-Tac. The system leverages tactile feedback from the proposed round-shaped sensor (R-Tac), integrated into a dexterous robotic hand, to grasp thin, deformable, paper-like objects. (a) Hand motions are generated by a diffusion-based policy inspired by human strategies, such as sliding and pinching. (b) The hardware setup includes a robotic arm, a dexterous hand, and four fingertip-mounted tactile sensors that simultaneously detect force and slip events.
figures.caption_ko: PP-Tac 시스템 개요. 제안한 원형 촉각 센서(R-Tac)의 촉각 피드백을 활용하여 얇고 변형 가능한 종이 유사 물체를 다지 로봇 손으로 파지한다. (a) 슬라이딩·핀칭 같은 인간 전략에서 영감을 받은 확산 정책으로 손 동작을 생성한다. (b) 로봇 팔, 다지 손, 4개 손가락 끝의 촉각 센서로 구성된 하드웨어 설정.

figures.caption: The hardware design of the R-Tac and its integration into the four-fingered dexterous robotic hand system. (a) illustrates the pipeline of depth reconstruction. (b) illustrates the exploded view of the sensor, detailing each component. (c) shows the dimensions of the sensor. (d) shows the schematic design. (e) illustrates the robotic hand equipped with four sensors as its distal links.
figures.caption_ko: R-Tac 하드웨어 설계 및 4지 로봇 손 시스템 통합. (a) depth 재구성 파이프라인, (b) 센서 분해도 및 각 구성 요소, (c) 센서 치수, (d) 회로 설계, (e) 4개 센서를 원위 링크로 장착한 로봇 손.

figures.caption: Slip detection. The left tactile image shows no contact, while the middle tactile image highlights wrinkle features during slip. The network computes the probability of slipping Pslip using the no-contact tactile image and five most recent tactile images.
figures.caption_ko: 슬립 감지. 왼쪽 촉각 이미지는 접촉 없음, 중간 이미지는 슬립 중 주름 특징을 강조. 네트워크는 비접촉 촉각 이미지와 최근 5개 촉각 이미지를 이용해 슬립 확률 Pslip을 계산한다.

figures.caption: Force analysis during grasping flat objects. The grasping process is made possible by the following forces: (1) the contact normal force exerted by the sensor on the object. (2) the static friction force (f1, f'1) between fingers and the object, (3) a dynamic friction force (f2, f'2) between the object and the terrain. When the static friction (f1, f'1) exceeds the critical buckling resistance of the paper, the sheet deforms, creating a stable pinch region that facilitates successful grasping.
figures.caption_ko: 평면 물체 파지 중 힘 분석. (1) 센서가 물체에 가하는 법선력, (2) 손가락과 물체 사이의 정지 마찰력(f1, f'1), (3) 물체와 지형 사이의 동적 마찰력(f2, f'2). 정지 마찰력이 종이의 임계 좌굴 저항을 초과하면 시트가 변형되어 안정적인 핀치 영역이 형성된다.

figures.caption: Fingertip trajectories from data synthesis. Trajectories ensure fingertip sliding along the terrain surface. Adjusting the distance between waypoints and terrain affects sensor deformation. The right figure projects trajectories of two fingers onto the m-z and n-z planes, where m and n are straight-line projections of fingertip trajectories on the palm-aligned x-y plane, and the z-axis extends outward from the hand.
figures.caption_ko: 데이터 합성에서 생성된 손가락 끝 궤적. 궤적은 손가락 끝이 지형 표면을 따라 슬라이드하도록 보장한다. 오른쪽 그림은 두 손가락의 궤적을 m-z, n-z 평면에 투영한 것이다.

figures.caption: Inference pipeline of the proposed PP-Tac policy. Conditioned on robot proprioception and the target force that needs to be exerted, PP-Tac can infer the action of the next steps. If slip is detected between the finger and the flat object underneath, an incremental amount of force will be exerted by the finger.
figures.caption_ko: 제안한 PP-Tac 정책의 추론 파이프라인. 로봇 고유감각과 목표 접촉력을 조건으로 다음 행동을 추론한다. 손가락과 평면 물체 사이에 슬립이 감지되면 접촉력을 점진적으로 증가시킨다.

figures.caption: Reconstruction results. (a) Gallery of reconstructed depth and normal maps from tactile images. (b) Depth reconstruction error of indentation test.
figures.caption_ko: 재구성 결과. (a) 촉각 이미지에서 재구성된 depth 및 normal map 갤러리. (b) 압입 테스트의 depth 재구성 오차.

figures.caption: Gallery of Grasping Different Objects in Real-World Evaluations. This figure demonstrates successful grasps of five flat objects on four different types of terrains, highlighting the effectiveness of our hardware and the PP-Tac algorithm. (a) A paper sheet on a flat desktop. (b) A stiff kraft paper bag on a flat desktop. (c) A soft napkin on a plate. (d) A paper sheet on a randomly arranged book. (e) Paper sheet on a random terrain.
figures.caption_ko: 실환경 평가에서 다양한 물체 파지 갤러리. (a) 평평한 책상 위 종이, (b) 평평한 책상 위 크라프트 종이 가방, (c) 접시 위 냅킨, (d) 책 위 종이, (e) 불규칙 지형 위 종이.

figures.caption: Experimental Results. Evaluations were conducted to quantify the success rate of grasping four different flat objects (paper, plastic bag, cloth, and paper bag) across four terrain setups (plane, slope, book placement, and randomly generated complex terrain). Baseline conditions included: (1) Gripper*: grasp using a bi-finger gripper controlled by teleoperation; (2) Open-loop: baseline combines the PP-Tac-derived hand trajectory with compliant finger control via tactile feedback; (3) "Model based force tracking": combines the PP-Tac-derived hand trajectory with compliant finger control via tactile-feedback based force tracking.
figures.caption_ko: 실험 결과. 4개 평면 물체(종이, 비닐봉지, 천, 종이 가방)를 4개 지형(평면, 경사면, 책 배치, 무작위 복잡 지형)에서 파지하는 성공률 정량 평가. 베이스라인: (1) Gripper*: 원격 조작 이지 그리퍼, (2) Open-loop: PP-Tac 궤적 + 순종형 힘 제어, (3) Model based force tracking: PP-Tac 궤적 + 촉각 피드백 기반 힘 추적.

figures.caption: Example of establishing contact: First, the hand descends until a finger makes contact with the surface. A fixed-point rotation is performed around the contacting finger, as shown in (a). The hand then continues to rotate until a second finger makes contact, triggering a fixed-axis rotation around both contacting fingers, as shown in (b). The process is complete when three or more fingers are in contact, as shown in (c).
figures.caption_ko: 접촉 설정 예시: (a) 손이 내려가 한 손가락이 접촉하면 고정점 회전 수행, (b) 두 번째 손가락이 접촉할 때까지 회전 후 두 손가락 축 기반 고정축 회전, (c) 세 손가락 이상 접촉 시 완료.

references.title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
references.author: Chi et al.
references.description: Core learning framework adopted by PP-Tac for grasp motion generation. PP-Tac trains a diffusion-based policy on synthesized pinching trajectory datasets to control the hand-arm system end-to-end.

references.title: GelSight: High-Resolution Robot Tactile Sensors for Estimating Geometry and Force
references.author: Yuan et al.
references.description: Pioneering vision-based tactile sensor design that inspired R-Tac. PP-Tac builds upon gel-based optical tactile sensing to reconstruct depth/normal maps from deformed gel surface images.

references.title: UniDexGrasp: Universal Robotic Dexterous Grasping via Learning Diverse Proposal Generation and Goal-Conditioned Policy
references.author: Xu et al.
references.description: Representative dexterous grasping system for rigid objects. PP-Tac identifies the gap that such systems cannot handle thin deformable objects, motivating the tactile feedback and force control approach.
